title: lithocarpus inversus
side_effects: Lithocarpus inversus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps, due to its high content of tannins and saponins. In rare cases, prolonged consumption of Lithocarpus inversus may lead to kidney problems, as its high saponin content can cause damage to kidney cells. Additionally, individuals with allergies may experience skin irritation, such as hives and itching, after coming into contact with the plant's sap. Prolonged exposure to Lithocarpus inversus extracts may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.
